title: Overflow — when the counter runs out of room
problem: Count upward in a fixed 8-bit register (it can only hold 0 to 255) and see what happens past the top.

trace.note: count starts at 254. Pretend count lives in a fixed 8-bit register — it can only hold whole numbers from 0 to 255.
One tick: count is 255 — the maximum an 8-bit register can store (all eight bits set to 1).

trace.checkpoint_prompt: count is at 255 — the largest value an 8-bit register can hold. After `count = (count + 1) % 256` runs, what will count be?

trace.note: Tick again — and count rolls over to 0. There was no room for 256, so the carry fell off the end. This is OVERFLOW: a fixed-width counter wrapping past its maximum, like an odometer rolling 999 → 000.
It just keeps counting up from 0 again. The register has no memory that it overflowed — which is exactly why overflow bugs are so easy to miss.
